creator: florian.heigl@gmail.com
text:
Hi,

I had already written that http is the default, and I also explained it makes no sense to fetch this file via http.

The vhost also didn't come with a notice saying "oh no, don't use me to get this security-related information, I'm set up by mistake" :)


How come I had another URL than vuxml.freebsd.org?
Apparently the content of pkg.conf has at one time contained. 

VULNXML_SITE = "http://www.vuxml.org/freebsd/vuln.xml.bz2";

I had a quick check.
Both vuxml.org/freebsd and vuxml.freebsd.org go back to 2004.
the latter has redirected to vuxml.org/freebsd/

I'm not really surprised to see another change in the pkg config files, but of course that's not your fault.

Close as you wish, but don't be surprised 

- insecure default lets users search for better solution
- annual config format / content changes
- unintentional vhosts
- misconfigured vhosts

sorry, but no suprise people run into trouble like that :)
